title: Vendor Security Incident Notification Receipt Tracking

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uplifted from contributing to partial: UC provides meaningful monitoring evidence for CIP-013-1 R3 Part 3.1.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NERC CIP CIP-013-1 R3 Part 3.1 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.13.66: Vendor Security Incident Notification Receipt Tracking.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.13.66' running on index=email sourcetype=vendor:bulletin or HEC JSON, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

spl:
index=email sourcetype=vendor:bulletin earliest=-90d severity IN ("high","critical")
| eval recv=strptime(received_at,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval ack_epoch=strptime(acknowledged_at,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval ack_h=round((ack_epoch-recv)/3600,2)
| where isnull(acknowledged_at) OR ack_h>72
| table bulletin_id, vendor, received_at, acknowledged_at, ack_h
description: We log inbound vendor security bulletins and track acknowledgement SLAs for incidents that may affect BES software or services.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NERC CIP compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes email and sourcetypes vendor:bulletin, vendor:bulletin`. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Parse vendor mailing lists into structured events. (2) Define acknowledgement workflow in ITSM. (3) Alert procurement and security. (4) Link to CIP-008 if operational impact. Indexes required: `index=email`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=vendor:bulletin`, `sourcetype=vendor:bulletin``.
visualization: Table (late acks), Single value (open critical), Timeline. Key fields: `ack_epoch`, `ack_h`, `acknowledged_at`, `bulletin_id`, `received_at`, `recv`.
knownFalsePositives: Planned maintenance, registered change tickets, and approved work can look like noncompliance in raw telemetry; confirm against the current change record before you escalate.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=email` `sourcetype=vendor:bulletin` or HEC JSON.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Parse vendor mailing lists into structured events. (2) Define acknowledgement workflow in ITSM. (3) Alert procurement and security. (4) Link to CIP-008 if operational impact.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=email sourcetype=vendor:bulletin earliest=-90d severity IN ("high","critical")
| eval recv=strptime(received_at,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval ack_epoch=strptime(acknowledged_at,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval ack_h=round((ack_epoch-recv)/3600,2)
| where isnull(acknowledged_at) OR ack_h>72
| table bulletin_id, vendor, received_at, acknowledged_at, ack_h
```

Understanding this SPL

**Vendor Security Incident Notification Receipt Tracking (CIP-013-1 R3 Part 3.1)** — We log inbound vendor security bulletins and track acknowledgement SLAs for incidents that may affect BES software or services.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=email` `sourcetype=vendor:bulletin` or HEC JSON. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (263). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: email; **sourcetype**: vendor:bulletin. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=email, sourcetype=vendor:bulletin, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **recv** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **ack_epoch** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **ack_h** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where isnull(acknowledged_at) OR ack_h>72` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: ack_epoch, ack_h, acknowledged_at, bulletin_id, received_at, recv, vendor.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the ESP firewall log, Windows Security, or the asset register—whichever the search is built to mirror. Spot-check a few source events, reconcile counts, and verify field extractions, thresholds, and index permissions for the people who need this evidence.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (late acks), Single value (open critical), Timeline
grandmaExplanation: We log inbound vendor security bulletins and track acknowledgement SLAs for incidents that may affect BES software or services. That is important for the big power grid, where proof needs to line up with what the rules expect.
